sourceMovie.title: Joker
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Joker (2019) as a crime theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The crime and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: American Psycho
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Joker who respond to its crime will find American Psycho (2000) immediately familiar. Both films share a thriller sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Promising Young Woman
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Joker fans keep landing on Promising Young Woman (2020) — the crime is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: I Care a Lot
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: I Care a Lot draws from the same crime well as Joker, channeling thriller in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2021 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: The Iceman
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Iceman (2012) captures the crime appeal of Joker without simply imitating it. The thriller streak running through both films is what puts The Iceman at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Sexy Beast
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The crime appeal of Joker — that dark edge, the thriller — translates cleanly into Sexy Beast (2001). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Juice
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Juice (1992) clearly understood what makes crime work — the same quality that keeps Joker rewatchable. Expect the same dark rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Gloria
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Gloria (1980) is the kind of film Joker fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The crime overlap is real, but it's the dark emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: The Batman
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Batman shares Joker's gift for crime — the 2022 film delivers thriller without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Batman: The Killing Joke
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Joker compelling — the crime, the drama — shows up in Batman: The Killing Joke (2016) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that uplifting storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: The Outsiders
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The crime energy of Joker is alive in The Outsiders (1983), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a tense payoff that rivals the original pick.

faq.question: What should I watch after Joker?
faq.answer: Try American Psycho, Promising Young Woman, I Care a Lot—each shares DNA with Joker while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Joker?
faq.answer: Joker (2019) is best described as Crime, Thriller, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Joker part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Joker Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
